lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The ER floor at this hour: controlled velocity. He reads the board without approaching it. Three admitted, two waiting, one discharge pending. Nothing about the diverted patient — that name belongs to a different corridor now, a different chart system, a different set of eyes.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He finds Nurse Chen at the supply station and keeps his voice below the ambient noise.

lines.speaker: THEO OKAFOR
lines.text: The patient from bay three. Last Tuesday. Transferred up to 4-North.

lines.speaker: THEO OKAFOR
lines.text: No. But you might have heard.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She looks at him the way people look when they understand exactly what's being asked and exactly why the asker cannot ask it through official channels.

lines.speaker: NURSE CHEN
lines.text: Stable this morning. That's what I heard.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He nods. Stable is not the same as being where the right care lives. Stable covers a wide territory. Stable is what a building says when it wants you to stop asking questions.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He takes a chart. Writes nothing in it. Returns it to the rack. The bay where that patient had been is occupied now — a man with his shoes still on, jacket folded across his chest, not yet processed. The ER absorbs. That's what it does.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The complaint is where he left it. Four pages, folded once, the top edge worn from being opened and refolded across twelve days. He has read it enough times to recite the paragraphs without looking. He reads them anyway.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Bay closures: documented. Diversion incidents: documented. Staffing ratios at time of each incident: documented. The name of the patient from February: documented. The name of the patient now on 4-North: blank. A space that already knows its own shape.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He reads to the signature line. He has read to the signature line seventeen times. He counted, because that's what you do when a decision refuses to arrive on its own — you turn it into arithmetic and wait for the number to tell you something.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: An unfiled complaint is still yours. It carries your name and no one else's weight. File it, and the building takes it. The building has taken things before and called them procedures, internal reviews, matters under consideration. It absorbed the first death and the shift continued.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He folds it back along the crease. Breast pocket. Returns to the floor before the thought finishes itself.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: A new call comes in. He takes the marker. Updates the status column. The work closes around him — completely, without asking.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Above him, four floors up, a man is stable. Stable, and where the right care isn't.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He will date the form tonight. He has been telling himself this for twelve days. Tonight it sits differently in his chest. He cannot explain why. The shift continues.
